description_long: This is a two-story traditional Japanese restaurant with a tiled gable roof and a wooden frame structure. The facade features sliding glass doors on the ground floor, partially covered by a blue noren curtain with white Japanese motifs, and a bamboo blind above. The upper floor has a large window with a bamboo shade and a sign with Japanese characters. The exterior walls are white with visible weathering and patches, and the building is accented by dark wooden beams. Air conditioning units are mounted on the rear and side walls, and a small awning extends over the entrance. The overall appearance is rustic and authentic, evoking a classic neighborhood eatery.
description: A two-story traditional Japanese restaurant with a tiled roof, sliding glass doors, bamboo blinds, and a blue noren curtain at the entrance.
description_view_0: Front view showing sliding glass doors, a blue noren curtain, bamboo blinds, and a sign with Japanese characters.
description_view_1: Side view with a plain white wall, bamboo blind, weathered patches, and part of the blue noren curtain visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying two air conditioning units, weathered wall patches, and the tiled roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a single air conditioning unit, a small awning, and a mostly plain wall.
attribute_signage: A rectangular sign with Japanese characters is displayed on the upper front facade, and a blue noren curtain with white Japanese motifs hangs at the entrance.
attribute_facade_design: The facade features a traditional Japanese design with a tiled gable roof, dark wooden beams, and white plaster walls. Sliding glass doors are present on the ground floor, partially covered by a blue noren curtain, while bamboo blinds and a sign with Japanese characters add authentic detail. The exterior is weathered, with visible patches and a rustic, inviting appearance.
